title: Remote/chat invites arrive live; consent before WebRTC (dbg138)
description:
After dbg137, Mike could send remote and chat invites but Kat often only saw them after a hard refresh. When she did see activity, the support widget showed "establishing control channel" instead of the Accept/Decline modal, while Mike's panel said "Connected to Kat" but still waited for accept — WebRTC never completed.

Root causes: (1) Support init was deferred up to 3s on requestIdleCallback for customers too, so SSE and invite polling started late and could miss events. Chat had no poll fallback at all — only SSE. (2) Orphan "active" remote rows in Postgres (prior sessions not ended) made the customer auto-attach WebRTC without consent while the admin waited on a new pending invite for a different session id. (3) Admin UI opened the remote panel as "Connected to …" even when only a pending invite existed.

This release: customers boot support immediately; 5s poll for pending remote + chat invites; SSE reconnects faster when auth token was late; auto-restore active remote only when the stored persist token matches (proves prior accept); backend ends stale sessions when a new invite is created and prefers pending over orphan active in /remote/active; admin polls for accept when SSE is missed; remote panel shows "Waiting for …" until connected.

Who is affected: Horizon admins and customers using PipeShare/PipeSync remote support and chat on the OVH private server. SaaS when Mike applies the platform version in releases.html.
changeLog: Customer support init runs immediately for non-admin users (SSE + invite poll start sooner).
5s poll: pending remote via GET /remote/active and pending chat via new GET /chat/pending.
Customer auto-reconnects WebRTC only when local persist token matches session (no orphan attach).
Backend ends superseded remote sessions on new invite; /remote/active prefers pending over stale active.
Admin polls for customer accept when remote-response SSE is missed; panel title shows waiting state.
SSE client: faster token retry, reconnect on tab visible, hello event wired.
